name: Griseofulvin
description: An oral fungistatic pharmaceutical. Binds to fungal microtubules and keratin precursor cells, making them highly resistant to fungal invasion and successfully treating severe dermatophyte scalp infections (tinea capitis).
preparation: Oral administration of 500mg daily. Must be taken with fat-rich foods or milk to dramatically optimize systemic absorption.
safetyAlert: May cause headache, gastrointestinal upset, and mild hepatotoxicity. Monitor liver function during long-term regimens.
interactions: Alcohol increases griseofulvin absorption but accelerates central nervous system depression. Reduces effectiveness of oral contraceptives.
